title: Cyrtandra natewaensis

The Gesneriaceae family consists of over 1,500 species of flowering plants, commonly known as African violets or gesneriads. These plants are native to tropical regions around the world, particularly in Africa and Asia. The classification of Cyrtandra natewaensis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Gesneriaceae family.

intro: Cyrtandra natewaensis, a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, is a rare and endemic plant found in the Natewa Swamp Forest in Fiji. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its purported antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Cyrtandra natewaensis include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in traditional medicine and is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and in some cases, its extracts are also used to make topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin care.
